normalized_title: State Planning Policy 7.0 Design of the Built Environment
version_date: February 2019 (Version 1 published; text states 'Version 1 published February 2019')
scope_summary: Overarching State Planning Policy made under Part 3 of the Planning and Development Act 2005 addressing design quality and built form outcomes across Western Australia. It applies to all levels of the planning hierarchy - activity centre plans, structure plans, local development plans, subdivision, development applications and public works - and establishes ten Design Principles (Schedule 1), design review processes and design skills/design statement requirements. It is the parent framework for supporting design SPPs such as SPP 7.2 and the design-quality aspects of the Residential Design Codes.
definitions: Design Principles (Schedule 1: context and character; landscape quality; built form and scale; functionality and build quality; sustainability; amenity; legibility; safety; community; aesthetics)
design statement (content described in s6: explanation of how the proposal addresses the Design Principles)
